title: tetragonia moorei
intro: Tetragonia moorei, also known as New Zealand spinach or kōkihi, is a perennial herb native to New Zealand and Australia, belonging to the Aizoaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate digestive issues, and act as a natural diuretic. The health benefits of Tetragonia moorei include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce the risk of heart disease, and provide essential nutrients such as iron and calcium. In herbal preparations, Tetragonia moorei can be consumed as a raw leafy green, used in teas, or dried and powdered for later use in soups, stews, and other dishes.
